model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the CLICK ME button
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (638,124) said dx=-198 dy=206 conf=0.30 -> (440,330)
  iter 1: at (440,330) said dx=-198 dy=206 conf=0.30 -> (242,536)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.content:
The previous iterations suggest that the model is consistently overshooting the target to the bottom-left.

The current crosshair is at (242, 536). The "CLICK ME" button appears to be around (440, 330) based on the first iteration's reported offset of dx=-198, dy=206.

To move from (242, 536) to (440, 330), we need to move right by 440 - 242 = 198 pixels and up by 330 - 536 = -206 pixels.

Given the consistency of the previous reported offsets, it's likely the model is still a bit off. I'll report an offset that attempts to correct the overshooting, with a moderate confidence to allow for further refinement.
